title: glechoma mallophora
intro: Glechoma mallophora, also known as creeping gypsywort, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Glechoma mallophora include relief from coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Glechoma mallophora include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
